user.title: Setup SmartCharge
user.description: Choose whether you want to configure a car or a charging station

car.title: Configure Car
car.description:
Provide the entities used to track charging and the electricityMap API key.

{api_key_help}

station.title: Configure Charging Station
station.description: Choose how you'd like to configure your charging station

temporary.title: Temporary Charging Station
temporary.description: Configure a one-off charging stop. This station will be automatically removed once your car's odometer increases from its current value.

wallbox.title: Custom Wallbox
wallbox.description: Add a wallbox at the car's current location. Uses live entity values for CO2 intensity and energy price.

auto_find.title: Find Current Charging Station
auto_find.description: Search for an EnBW charging station at the car's current GPS location. The search expands from 50 m to 200 m if no station is found immediately.

pick_car.title: Select Car
pick_car.description: Select which car to use for this configuration.

integration_discovery.title: Charging Station Discovered
integration_discovery.description: A charging station was found near your car. Select a station to add it to SmartCharge.

search_map.title: Search Nearby Stations
search_map.description:
Drag the pin on the map to the area where your charging station is located. Adjust the radius to cover the search area.

The map defaults to your Home Assistant home location.

select_station.title: Select Charging Station

browse_map.title: Browse EnBW Charging Map
browse_map.description:
Open the EnBW charging station map to find your station ID:

[EnBW Charging Map]({map_url})

To find the station ID, open your browser's developer tools (F12), go to the Network tab, and click a station on the map. Look for a request to chargestations/XXXXXX — the number is the station ID.

enter_station_id.title: Enter Station ID
enter_station_id.description:
Enter the numeric station ID from the EnBW API URL.

To find it: open the [EnBW map]({map_url}), press F12 → Network tab, click a station, and look for the number in the chargestations/XXXXXX request URL.

Example: 986728

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure SmartCharge
reconfigure.description: Update the configuration for this entry.

data.auto_discovery: Automatically discover charging stations while charging

configure_settings.title: Configure Settings
configure_settings.description:
Configure how often to fetch data from the API and API key settings

{update_interval_description}

data.manual_api_key: Manual API key (only used when auto-renewal is off)
data.static_friendly_name: Custom Friendly Name (leave blank for default)

error.connection_error:
Failed to connect to EnBW API. Please check:
- Your internet connection
- The station ID is correct
- Try again in a moment
error.invalid_station: Station not found. Please verify the station ID on the EnBW map.
error.no_charge_points_selected: Please select at least one charge point.
error.missing_fields: Please fill in all required fields.
error.no_stations_found: No charging stations found in this area. Try increasing the radius or moving the pin.
error.no_location: Please select a location on the map.
error.unknown_error: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.
error.gps_unavailable: Car GPS location is unavailable. Make sure the device tracker is reporting a valid position.
error.no_stations_found_nearby: No charging stations found within 200 m of the car's current location.
error.no_car_entries: No car entries found. Please add a car first.

abort.no_car_entries: No car entries found. Please add a car first.

data.manual_api_key: Manual API key (only used when auto-renewal is off)
data.auto_discovery: Automatically discover charging stations while charging
